steps.title: Translate the words to an equation
steps.content: Antonia pays a flat $\$2$ for the pasta and $\$1.30$ for each pound of tomatoes. If $p$ is the number of pounds of tomatoes, the cost of the tomatoes is $1.30p$ dollars.

steps.title: Add the two costs to the total
steps.content: Total spent = cost of tomatoes + cost of pasta: $$1.30p+2=7.20.$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $1.30p+2=7.20$ — pasta ($\$2$) plus tomatoes ($1.30p$) equals total ($\$7.20$). Keep.
  • B) $1.30p-2=7.20$ — subtracts the pasta cost; this would mean pasta reduced the total, which is wrong. Eliminate.
  • C) $1.30+2p=7.20$ — treats $p$ as pounds of pasta at $\$2$/lb and tomatoes as a flat $\$1.30$, reversing the per-pound and flat costs. Eliminate.
  • D) $1.30-2p=7.20$ — subtracts a cost term and swaps the rates; gives a negative $p$. Eliminate.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
